title: ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator wiring the loop eval to the HBR test-packet corpus
goal: Implement the Evaluator that the loop core (MT-148) consumes per Q-SELF-IMPROVE-TARGET: validator first-pass PASS rate on the fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus. The evaluator runs the validator against each corpus item under a frozen sandbox (cluster B SandboxAdapter) and returns per-split PASS rates plus auxiliary metrics needed for the multi-metric promotion floor (latency p95, capsule bytes).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/evaluator.rs (ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator + EvalResult + SplitMetrics + ValidatorVerdict types). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/validator_first_pass_evaluator_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-148 (loop core) + MT-150 (corpus + split). Cluster B SandboxAdapter is consumed by trait (cross-cluster integration boundary).
implementation_notes: Per Q-SELF-IMPROVE-TARGET operator decision: 'validator first-pass PASS rate on fixed ~30-item HBR test-packet corpus'. Per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS: 'multi-metric promotion floor (dev PASS up AND latency p95 not regressed AND capsule bytes not regressed AND holdout PASS rate not regressed)'. Surface: `pub struct ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator<'a> { sandbox: &'a dyn LoopSandbox, validator_runner: &'a dyn ValidatorRunner }` with `pub fn evaluate(split: &CorpusSplit, key_provider: &dyn KeyProvider, snapshot: &EditableSurfaceSnapshot) -> Result<EvalResult, EvalError>`. EvalResult: `{ train: SplitMetrics, dev: SplitMetrics, holdout: SplitMetrics, evaluated_at_utc, snapshot_hash: String }`. SplitMetrics: `{ pass_rate: f64 (0.0..=1.0), pass_count: u32, total_count: u32, latency_p95_ms: u64, capsule_bytes_p95: u64, per_item_results: Vec<PerItemResult> }`. PerItemResult: `{ item_id, verdict: ValidatorVerdict, latency_ms, capsule_bytes, error: Option<String> }`. Evaluation runs each corpus item through ValidatorRunner inside a sandboxed instance (so the editable surface snapshot is the ONLY change between baseline and trial runs); seed isolation per RW-6. Holdout evaluation decrypts via KeyProvider, runs items, re-encrypts holdout records before returning (holdout never leaves memory in plaintext form persistently). Tests: evaluator computes pass_rate correctly from per-item PASS/FAIL; latency p95 computed via standard order-statistic; capsule bytes p95 from injection records; holdout decryption fails cleanly without key; evaluator records snapshot_hash so EvalResult is traceable to its surface snapshot.
risk_if_missed: Without a typed evaluator the multi-metric promotion floor (MT-152) has nothing to gate against; the loop cannot measure improvement.
validator_focus: Evaluator is pure relative to sandbox + validator (both trait-injected). Per-split metrics computed independently. Holdout decryption is scoped to the eval call.

implementation_record.summary: MT-151 ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator implementation is on disk (src/self_improve/evaluator.rs, prior session, 311 lines: ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator + ValidatorRunner trait + EvalResult + SplitMetrics + PerItemResult + EvalError + 3 inline tests covering pass_rate/p95 computation, holdout decryption failure without key, p95 helper on small input). This session added the integration test file tests/validator_first_pass_evaluator_tests.rs (17 tests) that the contract owned_files names, covering: sandbox-invoked-exactly-once-per-evaluate, sandbox-failure-propagates-and-skips-runner, all-pass produces unit pass_rate, all-fail produces zero pass_rate, Skip verdict counts as not-pass, latency p95 lands on the correct nearest-rank order statistic, capsule_bytes p95 independent of latency, snapshot_hash deterministic + collision-sensitive + variant-kind-distinguishing, runner-observes-every-item-exactly-once-per-split (proves no double-evaluation + no missing item), wrong-key-yields-Corpus-error, validator runner failure propagates typed error, EvalResult round-trip via serde JSON, repeated-evaluate-deterministic-in-metrics, evaluated_at_utc non-decreasing across calls, KeyProvider::UnknownKey distinct from AEAD AuthFailure, split sizes match MT-150 60/20/20 + pairwise disjoint across train/dev/holdout.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — evaluator.rs is a complete impl with no '*Unavailable' / 'pending MT-NNN' / 'deferred to follow-on' patterns. Eval pipeline is end-to-end exercised through public Evaluator trait.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — integration tests exercise the public ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator + Evaluator trait via real ChaCha20-Poly1305 holdout decryption from MT-150 corpus.rs, real LoopSandbox trait (stubbed but typed), real ValidatorRunner trait (stubbed but typed). p95 computation tested against a known distribution and against the nearest-rank formula derived from the actual split member latencies.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — evaluator.rs authored in prior session; integration tests authored in this session; both differ from any validator role. Implementation_record explicitly marked as supporting evidence only.

implementation_files_verified_unchanged: src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/evaluator.rs (prior session, 311 lines, 3 inline tests; verified unchanged + 3/3 pass via cargo test --lib self_improve::evaluator)

red_team_coverage.sandbox_adapter_isolates_editable_surfac: covered — mt151_sandbox_runs_exactly_once_per_evaluate asserts sandbox.run() fires exactly once via CountingSandbox; mt151_sandbox_failure_propagates_and_skips_validator_runs asserts that when sandbox.run returns LoopSandboxError, evaluate returns EvalError::Sandbox and the validator runner records zero observed items (proving the sandbox short-circuit precedes any per-item evaluation).
red_team_coverage.holdout_decryption_scoped_to_single_eval: covered — evaluator.rs source code shows holdout_items goes out of scope at the end of evaluate(); mt151_wrong_key_for_holdout_yields_corpus_error + mt151_unknown_key_id_distinguishes_from_auth_failure prove the decryption only happens inside evaluate() with the caller-supplied KeyProvider. Plaintext leaves only as Vec<CorpusItem> consumed by evaluate_split and the resulting PerItemResult records contain item_id + verdict + latency_ms + capsule_bytes — no plaintext packet body bleeds into EvalResult.
red_team_coverage.latency_and_capsule_bytes_p95_computed_f: covered — mt151_latency_p95_is_correct_order_statistic feeds 12 train-split items with latencies derived from item ordinals, sorts them, and asserts latency_p95_ms matches sorted[ceil(0.95*n)-1]. mt151_capsule_bytes_p95_is_independent_of_latency varies capsule_bytes while holding latency constant and asserts both p95 metrics are derived independently. Together they refute any running-average implementation.

adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum.snapshot_hash_collision_sensitivity: mt151_snapshot_hash_distinguishes_variant_kinds — RetrievalPolicy vs ModelManual variants produce distinct SHA-256 hashes even for canonically-similar contents; guards against any flatten-to-string serialization bug.
adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum.split_disjointness_invariant: mt151_split_sizes_match_60_20_20_contract — asserts pairwise BTreeSet::is_disjoint across train/dev/holdout per_item_results, proving no item appears in two splits at once.
adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum.runner_observes_every_item_exactly_once: mt151_runner_observes_every_item_exactly_once_per_split — RecordingRunner records every observed item id; asserts the multiset equals train + dev + decrypted-holdout ids, with no duplicates or omissions.
adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum.serde_audit_round_trip: mt151_eval_result_round_trips_via_serde_json — full EvalResult round-trips, proving auditors can persist + replay.
adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum.key_provider_error_taxonomy: mt151_unknown_key_id_distinguishes_from_auth_failure — UnknownKey vs AuthFailure are distinct error variants; callers can route differently.
adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum.skip_verdict_semantics: mt151_skip_verdict_counts_as_not_pass — Skip is explicitly excluded from pass_count, defending against any future change that conflates Skip with Pass.

residual_risks: ValidatorRunner is the cross-cluster integration boundary; real production wiring (B-cluster SandboxAdapter + the actual validator first-pass runner) is not exercised by this MT and is a separate downstream MT.

Sandbox isolation is verified at the trait level (LoopSandbox trait + sandbox_run_id propagation) but the actual Cluster B SandboxAdapter implementation is consumed via trait, not the concrete impl; a wrong concrete adapter could silently fail to isolate without tripping these tests.

Holdout plaintext drop is verified by inspecting the source-code scope of holdout_items; there is no runtime memory-zeroization assertion. A future hardening MT could add explicit drop + zeroize() semantics.
The MT-148 LoopCore.execute_eval pathway is not exercised by this MT (it would require a LoopIteration in stage ExecuteEval); proven only at trait-implementation level.
No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-151.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Evaluator runs every item through the SandboxAdapter so editable surface changes are isolated from the host process.
Holdout decryption is scoped to a single eval call; plaintext is dropped after metrics are computed.
Latency and capsule-bytes p95 are computed from raw per-item observations, not from running averages.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator runs train + dev + holdout (decrypted in-process and dropped) through a sandboxed ValidatorRunner, computes proper p95 latency / capsule_bytes, returns SplitMetrics; snapshot_hash binds the eval to its candidate snapshot.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: ValidatorFirstPassEvaluator runs train + dev + holdout (decrypted in-process and dropped) through a sandboxed ValidatorRunner, computes proper p95 latency / capsule_bytes, returns SplitMetrics; snapshot_hash binds the eval to its candidate snapshot.
